intro: Superwall and Dable sit on opposite sides of the transparency line — Superwall sells a subscription from $10 with a free tier, while Dable only offers custom / contact-sales pricing. Superwall is remote-configurable paywalls and paywall A/B testing for iOS/Android subscription apps. Dable is native content-recommendation ad network, strong in Asia. Pick Superwall when the job is mobile, apps and you accept medium lock-in; pick Dable when it is content publishers with significant kr, jp, or southeast asian audience and you accept high lock-in. The data we have shows Superwall at mostly transparent pricing and docs with a polished developer experience, and Dable at opaque pricing and product information with average developer experience. The honest trade-off: neither is universal — Superwall is a poor fit for subscription-only apps, and Dable is a poor fit for western publishers or apps needing video, rewarded, or banner formats. Match the pricing model and lock-in level to how your team actually works, not the feature list.
quick_take: Superwall is for remote-configurable paywalls and paywall A/B testing; Dable is for native content-recommendation ad network; decide based on whether you can accept sales-call pricing.
verdict_a: Choose Superwall if a subscription at $10 and you want an entry-level free tier fits the budget and the work maps to remote-configurable paywalls and paywall A/B testing for iOS/Android subscription apps.
verdict_b: Choose Dable if deep integration is an acceptable price for the depth it buys and the work lines up with native content-recommendation ad network, strong in Asia.

faq.a: We can't verify it cleanly: Superwall starts at $10 (subscription), while Dable (custom) doesn't publish an entry price. Request a quote from Dable before assuming either side is cheaper.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Superwall to Dable?
faq.a: Moving from Superwall to Dable is feasible, but Dable is rated high lock-in — expect your integrations and workflows to need rework to match its model. Budget for a parallel-run period rather than a hard cutover.

Superwall scores higher on developer experience in our data (5/5 vs 3/5). That reflects public feedback on docs, onboarding, and day-to-day ergonomics. Still, run a spike with your own code — DX ratings don't capture every edge.

faq.q: Is Dable a good alternative to Superwall?
faq.a: Yes — Dable is a reasonable alternative to Superwall for mobile, apps. The practical differences are subscription-vs-custom billing and medium-vs-high lock-in. If those fit your constraints better, treat Dable as a credible swap.
